schemas.headline: Social Card Preview — Test Open Graph & Social Media Cards
schemas.description: Free interactive Social Card preview tool. Preview how your URL will look when shared on Facebook, Twitter, LinkedIn, Discord, and Slack.

mainEntity.name: What size should an Open Graph image be?

acceptedAnswer.text: The recommended og:image size is 1200 × 630 pixels (1.91:1 aspect ratio). This works across Facebook, Twitter/X, LinkedIn, Slack, and Discord. Minimum size is 600 × 315px, but 1200 × 630 gives the best quality on high-DPI screens.

mainEntity.name: How do I check if my og:image is working?

acceptedAnswer.text: Enter your URL in our OG Image Preview tool above. It fetches your page's Open Graph meta tags and shows exactly how your link will appear when shared on social media. You can also use Facebook's Sharing Debugger or Twitter's Card Validator.

mainEntity.name: What happens if I don't have an og:image tag?

acceptedAnswer.text: Without an og:image tag, social platforms will either show no image, auto-select an image from your page (often the wrong one), or display a generic placeholder. Always set og:image explicitly for control over how your links appear when shared.
